steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $\frac{9}{ \left(x+1\right) \left(4x-5\right)}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Find a common denominator for $\frac{4}{4x-5} - \frac{1}{x+1}$. The LCD is $(4x-5)(x+1)$.
